reasoning: The Item 19 pro-forma operating statements are based on 11,332 independent franchisee traditional restaurants whose royalties were at 4% of Gross Sales. However, new franchisees entering the system after January 1, 2024, pay a 5% royalty rate (per Item 6, Footnote 2). This creates a 100 basis point (1 percentage point) difference. The OIBOC figures in the pro-forma are therefore overstated for prospective new franchisees by the additional 1% royalty that they will pay but which is not reflected in the historical cohort's costs. At the all-traditional average sales volume of $4,002,000, this represents approximately $40,020 per year in additional royalty burden not captured in the pro-forma.
quote_fragments: The royalties payable by these franchised restaurants during the covered period were 4% of Gross Sales.
The royalty of 5% of Gross Sales applies on and after January 1, 2024, in the following scenarios: (i) a franchisee opens a new restaurant
